rationale: A0 shows reg.exe writing a batch file path (C:\Users\user-01\AppData\Local\Temp\art.bat) to the UserInitMprLogonScript registry value under the user's environment hive, which is a well-known persistence mechanism that executes the specified script at every user logon. EV-001 confirms the explicit command used to set this value, run at High integrity from cmd.exe, targeting HKCU\Environment\UserInitMprLogonScript. The payload resides in a Temp directory — a common staging location for malicious scripts — and the registry key name 'art.bat' is consistent with Atomic Red Team simulation or actual adversary tooling. The combination of logon script persistence via a Temp-resident batch file is a clear indicator of malicious intent.
